lens_findings.finding: judge-accessibility (5): The visible five-step links, numbered sections, order total, and concise safety copy made the next action apparent. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): All five links changed scroll position; keyboard Tab then Enter on the skip link focused checkout-form. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (4): One-page entry still asks for many fields, but synthetic exemplar text, input modes, and compact completed sections reduce recall and repeat work. | judge-human-use (4): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Invalid submit focused error-summary with an actionable 13-item, four-section summary; correction and completion preserved entered data. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Observed save status, section status/compact summaries, and local confirmation status with SYN-2048 were explicit. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Skip target focus, keyboard completion, visible focus/large controls, linked errors, no console errors, and the terminal semantic gate all support this score. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): 390x844 inspection showed full-width fields and large shipping, edit, and place-order targets; terminal mobile gate passed. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Manual safe save restored contact and address after reload while card number and security code were empty; confirmation SYN-2048 remained after reload without payment details. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (4): Immediate local section, save, validation, and confirmation feedback makes waiting unlikely; the long one-page form remains visually substantial. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Clear progress required an explicit confirmation and both Escape and Cancel preserved work before the affirmative clear path. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (4): Progressive compact summaries, review edits, and error grouping help, though the full five-section single page is dense before compaction. | judge-human-use (4): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-a.json

judge-accessibility (5): Five labeled links, the compact choice, review edits, and a visible progress/status treatment made controls easy to find. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json

judge-accessibility (4): Every step link scrolled to its section and edit controls returned focus to the right input, but activating the skip link produced no identified focused target in this run. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json

judge-accessibility (4): The same synthetic hints, compact option, and editable review avoid re-entry, while the one-page form still has a high initial field count. | judge-human-use (4): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json

judge-accessibility (5): Invalid submit focused error-summary and grouped 13 corrections by four sections while preserving entries; correction then completed. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json

judge-accessibility (5): Observed save status, completed-state feedback, shipping updates, and a local SYN-2048 confirmation provided clear state changes. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json

judge-accessibility (4): Terminal semantic and keyboard gates passed and no console errors occurred, but the observed skip activation did not yield a named focused target. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json

judge-accessibility (5): At 390x844, fields and action buttons were broad and clearly separated; terminal mobile-touch gate passed. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json

judge-accessibility (4): Safe fields restored and payment secrets cleared after reload, but the local confirmation was not present after a confirmation reload. | judge-human-use (4): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json

judge-accessibility (4): Immediate, local status and confirmation feedback communicate progress without a spinner or implied remote wait. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json
judge-accessibility (5): Clear confirmation, Escape, Cancel, and affirmative clear paths were all exercised successfully. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json

judge-accessibility (4): Progress feedback and per-section error grouping help orient users; the initial all-at-once layout remains information-dense. | judge-human-use (4): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-b.json

judge-accessibility (4): Core links, fields, shipping choices, compacting, and edits were discoverable, but there was no discoverable save or clear-progress control. | judge-human-use (4): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json

judge-accessibility (4): Step links and edit controls worked and moved focus to their inputs; skip activation did not expose an identified target in this run. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json

judge-accessibility (4): Automatic safe restoration and compact summaries reduce repeated entry, though users lose an explicit moment to choose when saved progress is stored. | judge-human-use (4): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json

judge-accessibility (5): Invalid submit focused a detailed, grouped error summary and preserved all entries; corrected canonical completion succeeded. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json

judge-accessibility (4): Section and order feedback are clear, but automatic saving is less explicit and there is no observed clear confirmation/status path. | judge-human-use (4): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json

judge-accessibility (4): All objective accessibility gates passed, controls completed by browser automation, and no console errors occurred; the skip-focus observation remains a reservation. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json

judge-accessibility (5): The 390x844 layout kept fields, radios, edits, and submission controls large and separated; the mobile gate passed. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json

judge-accessibility (4): Contact/address restored while card number and security code cleared on reload; confirmation itself was absent after confirmation reload. | judge-human-use (3): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json

judge-accessibility (4): Local validation and confirmation were immediate, and automatic resume minimizes explicit save steps. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json

judge-accessibility (2): No clear-progress control or confirmation was discoverable, so clear/cancel/Escape could not be offered or exercised. | judge-human-use (3): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json

judge-accessibility (4): Structured errors, compact completed sections, and review edits reduce orientation load; invisible auto-save/clear absence makes persistence less predictable. | judge-human-use (5): see judging/final/judge-human-use/candidate-c.json
